question: Which command runs the full automatic migration recipe for React 19?

options.explanation: No such package exists. The official tool is the codemod CLI.
The --migrate flag does not exist in npm. Installing a package and migrating code are separate steps.
create-react-app has no migration feature and is no longer recommended.

question: Which APIs were removed in React 19?

options.explanation: useState is a core React hook and was not removed.
The mount() method does not exist in React. The new API uses createRoot from react-dom/client.
The render() method is not part of the react package. Rendering is done through react-dom/client.
This is a browser API unrelated to React.

question: What does codemod NOT do automatically when migrating to React 19?

options.text: Rewrite forms from onSubmit to Actions

options.explanation: This is one of the standard codemod transformations.

options.text: Replace forwardRef with ref as prop
options.explanation: Codemod automatically removes the forwardRef wrapper.
This is a standard codemod transformation for function components.

question: Which React type no longer includes children by default in React 19?

options.explanation: Class components always required explicit children in their types.
ReactElement is a return value type, not a component type. It does not contain children in its typing.
HTMLAttributes are DOM element types that always contained children through React.DOMAttributes.
